project_summary: The Patient Data Management System project aims to design and implement a comprehensive database schema for managing patient information efficiently using Snowflake. This system will ensure data integrity, security, and enable efficient retrieval and reporting for healthcare providers, ultimately supporting better decision-making and operational efficiency.

sprints.sprint_objective: Establish the project foundation, define scope, and outline the core methodology for the Patient Data Management System.

mini_sprints.description: Define the project's significance within healthcare data management, its purpose, and the benefits of the Patient Data Management System.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Detailed Introduction and Project Overview sections of the Project Bible.

mini_sprints.description: List specific project objectives and clearly define what is included and excluded from the system's functionalities and components.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Documented Objectives and Scope sections, including system components like patients, doctors, appointments, and treatments.

mini_sprints.description: Describe the methods and approaches to be used for system design and implementation, covering data modeling, database design principles, and query optimization.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Comprehensive Methodology section outlining project steps from requirements gathering to deployment.

sprints.sprint_objective: Design the detailed and normalized database schema for efficient patient data management within Snowflake.

mini_sprints.description: Develop entity-relationship diagrams to identify key entities (Patients, Doctors, Appointments, Treatments) and their relationships.
mini_sprints.deliverable: ERD documentation for the Patient Data Management System.

mini_sprints.description: Define detailed table structures, including column names, data types, and descriptions for all identified entities.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Detailed table schemas for Patients, Doctors, Appointments, and Treatments.

mini_sprints.description: Specify primary keys, foreign keys, unique constraints, and appropriate indexes to ensure data integrity and optimize query performance.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Database Schema Design section with defined constraints and indexes.

sprints.sprint_objective: Develop and document efficient SQL queries for all data manipulation and retrieval needs of the system.

mini_sprints.description: Write SQL queries for inserting new records, updating existing information, and deleting data across all tables.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Set of DML (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) SQL scripts for patient, doctor, appointment, and treatment data.

mini_sprints.description: Develop SQL queries to retrieve specific patient information, appointment schedules, and treatment histories.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Set of DQL (SELECT) SQL scripts for various data retrieval scenarios.

mini_sprints.description: Create SQL queries designed to generate reports and support basic data analytics for decision-making.
mini_sprints.deliverable: SQL scripts for generating common reports (e.g., appointments by doctor, treatments by patient).

sprints.sprint_objective: Set up the Snowflake environment and deploy the designed database schema and objects.

mini_sprints.description: Provision necessary Snowflake resources, including warehouses, databases, and schemas, and configure user access.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Configured Snowflake environment with appropriate roles and access controls.

mini_sprints.description: Generate Data Definition Language (DDL) scripts for creating all tables, constraints, and indexes in Snowflake.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Complete DDL script for the entire database schema.

mini_sprints.description: Execute the generated DDL scripts in the Snowflake environment to create the database schema.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Deployed database schema in Snowflake, ready for data.

sprints.sprint_objective: Load initial sample data and perform comprehensive functional testing of the deployed schema and developed queries.

mini_sprints.description: Create realistic sample data for patients, doctors, appointments, and treatments to populate the database.
mini_sprints.deliverable: CSV or SQL insert scripts containing sample data.

mini_sprints.description: Load the generated sample data into the respective tables in the Snowflake environment.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Populated database tables with sample data.

mini_sprints.description: Execute all developed SQL queries (DML and DQL) against the loaded sample data to verify correctness and expected output.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Test reports confirming functional correctness of all SQL queries.

mini_sprints.description: Verify that all defined constraints (primary keys, foreign keys, unique constraints) are correctly enforced.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Validation report confirming data integrity rules are active and functioning.

sprints.sprint_objective: Evaluate the system's performance and implement security measures to protect patient data.

mini_sprints.description: Analyze and optimize SQL queries for performance and efficiency, leveraging Snowflake's capabilities.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Optimized SQL queries with documented performance improvements.

mini_sprints.description: Implement appropriate security measures such as role-based access controls, data encryption, and auditing.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Configured security policies and access controls in Snowflake.

mini_sprints.description: Identify potential risks (e.g., data security, performance issues) and describe strategies for mitigating them.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Risks and Mitigation section of the Project Bible.

sprints.sprint_objective: Complete all project documentation, summarize outcomes, and outline future enhancements.

mini_sprints.description: Compile all drafted sections (Introduction, Overview, Objectives, Scope, Methodology, Database Schema Design, SQL Queries, Implementation Plan, Expected Outcomes, Risks and Mitigation, Conclusion) into the final Project Bible.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Complete 'Patient Data Management System' Project Bible document.

mini_sprints.description: Document the expected outcomes and benefits of the project, such as improved efficiency and enhanced reporting capabilities.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Expected Outcomes section of the Project Bible.

mini_sprints.description: Summarize the project's impact and outline potential future enhancements and scalability options.
mini_sprints.deliverable: Conclusion section of the Project Bible, including future considerations.
